title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: The global (0) COMMITMENT (COMMIT) to reducing carbon emissions has led to a surge in green technology. Many countries are now (17) HEAVILY (HEAVY) investing in wind and solar power. This (18) TRANSITION (TRANSIT) away from fossil fuels is necessary for environmental protection. Scientists are working on (19) SOLUTIONS (SOLVE) to store energy more efficiently for later use. The (20) AVAILABILITY (AVAILABLE) of affordable renewable energy is crucial for developing nations. Despite some (21) DISAGREEMENT (AGREE) regarding the cost, the long-term benefits are (22) CLEARLY (CLEAR) superior. (23) ENVIRONMENTALISTS (ENVIRONMENT) argue that we must act now to prevent irreversible damage. The (24) SUCCESSFUL (SUCCESS) implementation of these policies will determine the future of our planet. By embracing innovation and sustainable practices, we can build a resilient energy infrastructure that supports both economic growth and ecological health, ensuring a cleaner and more stable world for the coming generations and beyond.

questions.explanation: Adverb required to modify the verb 'investing'; HEAVY → HEAVILY.
Noun with a negative prefix required; AGREE → DISAGREEMENT.
Adverb required to modify the adjective 'superior'; CLEAR → CLEARLY.
Plural noun for people required; ENVIRONMENT → ENVIRONMENTALISTS.
Adjective required to modify 'implementation'; SUCCESS → SUCCESSFUL.
